content.full_text: State of California Fair Political Practices Commission P.O. BOX 807 • SACRAMENTO, 95804 • • • 1100 K STREET BUILDING, SACRAMENTO, 95814 November 29, 1976 Senator John Stull State Capitol Sacramento, CA 95814 Dear Senator Stull: Thank you for your letter of November 12, 1976, regarding the procedure for exemption from campaign disclosure requirements. The exemption procedure is required by the ruling of the U.S. Supreme Court in the case of Buckley v. Valeo, 96 S.Ct. 612, decided in January 1976. The Supreme Court ruled that although campaign disclosure requirements are generally valid, it is unconstitutional to apply such requirements to candidates and committees representing new or minor parties if it can be shown that they or their contributors will be subject to serious harassment or reprisals if campaign disclosure is required. I am personally in agreement with you that the disclosure requirements should be applied equally across the board, but the ruling of the Supreme Court is final and the Fair Political Practices Commission has attempted to comply with the letter and the spirit of the Constitution, as it has been construed. The exemption for Mr. Miller, the Communist Labor Party candidate for school board in San Francisco, was a temporary exemption, comparable to a preliminary injunction issued by a court. A hearing on the request for a permanent exemption will be held in San Francisco on December 9. If you would like to participate in any way, please contact Kenneth Pinney at (916) 322-5901 for further information. A copy of the applicable regulation is enclosed. Although I regret that you disagree with the Commission's recent action, I appreciate your strong support of the disclosure requirements as expressed in your letter. Sincerely, Daniel H. Lowenstein Chairman DHL:cjb Enclosure cc: All Legislators TECHNICAL ASSISTANCE/ADMINISTRATION (916) 322-5660 • EXECUTIVE/LEGAL 322-5901 • ENFORCEMENT/CONFLICT OF INTEREST 322-6441 ORY 200 (REV. 5-64) FACE SHEET FOR FILING ADMINISTRATIVE REGULATIONS WITH THE SECRETARY OF STATE (Pursuant to Government Code Section 11350.1) RECEIVED FOR FILING MAY 19 1976 Office of Administrative Hearings ENDORSED APPROVED FOR FILING (Gov. Code 11353) MAY 10 1976 Office of Administrative Hearings Copy below is hereby certified to be a true and correct copy of regulations adopted, or amended, or an order of repeal by: FAIR POLITICAL PRACTICES COMMISSION (Agency) Date of adoption, amendment, or repeal: May 4, 1976 By: [illegible] Chairman (Title) DO NOT WRITE IN THIS SPACE DO NOT WRITE IN THIS SPACE ORDER ADOPTING REGULATIONS OF THE FAIR POLITICAL PRACTICES COMMISSION After proceedings had in accordance with the provisions of the Administrative Procedure Act (Government Code Title 2, Div. 3, Part 1, Ch. 4.5) and pursuant to the authority vested by Section 83112 of the Government Code, and to implement, interpret or make specific Sections 84102, 84210(g), 84210(h), 84210(j), 84210(k) and 84214 of the Government Code the Fair Political Practices Commission hereby adopts its regulation in Division VI, Title 2, California Administrative Code, as follows: Chapter 4: Campaign Disclosure Section 18429. Providing a procedure to obtain an exemption from reporting and dis- closure requirements (84102, 84210(g), 84210(h), 84210(j), 84210(k), 84214). (a) Any candidate or committee required by the Act to file a statement of organization or a campaign statement may apply to DO NOT WRITE IN THIS SPACE D. 400A (8.71) CONTINUATION SHEET FOR FILING ADMINISTRATIVE REGULATIONS WITH THE SECRETARY OF STATE (Pursuant to Government Code Section 11380.1) Commission for an exemption from some or all of the reporting obli- gations imposed by Government Code Sections 84102 and 84214 and sub- sections (g), (h), (j) and (k) of Section 84210 in accordance with the following procedure: (1) An application for an exemption shall be in writing shall include a short and plain statement of all material facts and of the reasons an exemption is sought, and shall list the sections and subsections of the Act from which the applicant seeks an exemption. (2) The Commission shall consider a request for an exemption at its first regular meeting following receipt of an application. If the application states facts which, if proved, would entitle the applicant to an exemption under the standards set forth in paragraph (a)(5), the Commission shall accept the matter for a hearing. The Chairman shall schedule the hearing to take place within 30 to 60 days of the Commission's action, provided, however, that the Chair- man may extend or shorten the time for good cause or pur- suant to a stipulation of the parties. (3) A hearing under this provision shall be conducted in accordance with the Administrative Procedure Act (Gov- ernment Code Sections 11500 et seq.). (4) The Commission, if requested, shall permit the Attorney General and any city attorney or district attorney - 2 - S1918-750 3-78 24M A . 400A (3-71) CONTINUATION SHEET FOR FILING ADMINISTRATIVE REGULATIONS WITH THE SECRETARY OF STATE (Pursuant to Government Code Section 11380.1) with enforcement jurisdiction relative to the applicant to become a party, within the meaning of Government Code Sections 11500 et seq., to a hearing held pursuant to this regulation. The Commission may also permit any other governmental entity or interested person to become a party upon application. A party shall be entitled and subject to the discovery procedures set forth in Govern- ment Code Sections 11507.5, 11507.6, 11507.7 and 11511 and may cross-examine witnesses. Any other person or governmental entity may present to the Commission testi- mony or evidence relevant to the hearing, which the Commission may elect to receive in either oral or written form, but shall not be permitted to cross-examine wit- nesses or participate in discovery. (5) The Commission shall grant a full, partial or conditional exemption from the Act's reporting and dis- closure requirements if it determines: (i) that the applicant has demonstrated, by a preponderance of the evidence, a reasonable probability that compelled dis- closure would subject the applicant or the applicant's supporters to threats, harassment or reprisals and (ii) that such threatened injury outweighs the state's in- terest in requiring full campaign disclosure. The Com- mission shall state the reasons for its decision in writing. - 3 - 31913-120 3-23 Tam △ FOR FILING ADMINSTRATIVE REGULATIONS WITH THE SECRETARY OF STATE (Pursuant to Government Code Section 11350.1) (6) The Commission shall set a term of not more than two years for an exemption granted pursuant to this regulatic (b) Prior to the Commission's final disposition of a request for an exemption, an applicant may submit a written appli- cation for a preliminary exemption in accordance with the following procedure: (1) An application for a preliminary exemption shall include a short and plain statement of the reasons a preliminary exemption is sought and shall be accompanied by affidavits or declarations that set forth supporting facts based on personal knowledge. (2) The Commission shall consider an application for a preliminary exemption at its first regular meeting following receipt of the application. An application received less than 30 days prior to an applicant's filing deadline will not be considered unless the applicant states good cause for not submitting the application sooner. If an applicant is required to comply with a filing obligation before the Commission's next regular meeting, the Commission shall consider the applicant's request for a preliminary exemption at a special meeting called pursuant to 2 Cal. Adm. Code Section 18315 if the Chairman or any two Commissioners find that good cause exists for the lateness of the application. When the Commission considers a request for a preliminary - 4 - 31810-750 3-73 244 △ FD. 400A (8-71) CONTINUATION SHEET FOR FILING ADMINISTRATIVE REGULATIONS WITH THE SECRETARY OF STATE (Pursuant to Government Code Section 11350.1) exemption, it also shall consider affidavits and declar- ations submitted in opposition to the request pursuant to paragraph (b)(4). (3) If an application for a preliminary exemption is timely filed or good cause for late filing is shown, the Commission shall grant the preliminary exemption if the affidavits and declarations submitted show a reasonable probability that all or part of the applicant's request for an exemption will ultimately be granted. The Com- mission shall state the reasons for its decision in writing. (4) Any person may object to a preliminary exemption, either before or after it is granted, by submitting to the Commission affidavits or declarations based on per- sonal knowledge. If a preliminary exemption has been granted and the Commission decides that all affidavits and declarations submitted show that there is not a rea- sonable probability that the applicant ultimately will receive an exemption, then the Commission shall withdraw the preliminary exemption. (5) A preliminary exemption granted pursuant to this regulation shall remain in effect until withdrawn or until the Commission finally disposes of the request for an exemption. (6) An applicant who receives a preliminary exemption shall, while the preliminary exemption is in effect, - 5 - OA (8-71) CONTINUATION SHEET FOR FILING ADMINSTRATIVE REGULATIONS WITH THE SECRETARY OF STATE (Pursuant to Government Code Section 11330.1) maintain such records as are necessary to permit the applicant to comply fully with the reporting obligations of the Act should the applicant's request for an exemp- tion ultimately be denied. (c) Upon receipt of an application for an exemption or a preliminary exemption, the Commission shall notify the Attorney General and both the district attorney and city attorney of the jurisdiction in which the applicant's residence, principal place of business, or principal office or headquarters is located. (d) Any interested person, a Commissioner, or the Executive Director or his or her designee may ask the Commission to withdraw an exemption granted pursuant to this regulation. Such a request shall be considered only if it is supported by affidavits or declarations, based on personal knowledge, which contain facts not presented at the hearing held to consider the applicant's most recent request for an exemption. The candidate or committee holding the exemption shall be notified of such request and given an opportunity to file counter affidavits or declarations. The Commission shall order that a further hearing be held to reconsider its issuance of an exemption if new facts are presented which demonstrate a strong likelihood: (i) that compelled disclosure will not expose the applicant or the applicant's supporters to threats, harassment or reprisals or, (ii) that even if there is a danger of such injury, the threatened harm is out- weighed by the state's interest in requiring full campaign dis- - 6 - 11810-370 3/23 24M 7D. 400A (S.71) CONTINUATION SHEET FOR FILING ADMINISTRATIVE REGULATIONS WITH THE SECRETARY OF STATE (Pursuant to Government Code Section 11380.1) closure. A hearing held pursuant to this paragraph to reconsider an exemption shall be de novo and shall be conducted in accordance with the procedures and standards set forth in paragraphs (a)(3), (a)(4), (a)(5) and (a)(6) of this regulation. (e) If the Commission grants an exemption or a prelim- inary exemption pursuant to this regulation, it shall so notify the officers with whom the applicant is required by Government Code Section 81005 to file a campaign statement. COMMENT: This regulation is adopted in order to establish procedures which will enable the Commission to interpret Chapter 4 of the Act in a manner consistent with the constitutional standards pronounced in Buckley v. Valeo, ____ U.S. ____,.96 S.Ct. 612, 658-61 (1976) and Doe v. Martin, 404 F. Supp. 753 (D.D.C. 1975). It is the intent of the Commission that exemptions will be granted pursuant to this regulation only to the extent required by the federal or the state constitution. * * * * * * * * This regulation will not create any new or increased costs to local government pursuant to Section 2231 of the Revenue and Taxation Code. - 7 - ROOM 2057 STATE CAPITOL SACRAMENTO 95814 (916) 445-3731 COMMITTEES VICE CHAIRMAN EDUCATION AGRICULTURE AND WATER RESOURCES INDUSTRIAL RELATIONS LOCAL GOVERNMENT California State Senate 220 WEST SECOND AVENUE ESCONDIDO 92025 (714) 743-8338 MEMBER EDUCATIONAL MANAGEMENT AND EVALUATION COMMISSION SELECT COMMITTEE ON SMALL BUSINESS ENTERPRISES CALIFORNIA ECONOMIC DEVELOPMENT COMMISSION SELECT COMMITTEE ON MARITIME INDUSTRY EDUCATION COMMISSION OF THE STATES 73-282 HIGHWAY 111, SUITE 106 PALM DESERT 92260 (714) 346-7738 895 BROADWAY EL CENTRO 92343 (714) 353-4038 JOHN STULL SENATOR THIRTY-EIGHTH DISTRICT SAN DIEGO, RIVERSIDE AND IMPERIAL COUNTIES November 12, 1976 Mr. Daniel Lowenstein, Chairman Fair Political Practices Commission Post Office Box 807 Sacramento, California 95804 Dear Mr. Lowenstein: Having just learned of the Commission's decision to exempt a candidate from the reporting requirements of Government Code Section 84210(g) and 84214, I would appreciate an explanation, and I would like to know the source of your authority to grant such exemptions. Does the public's "right to know," one of the chief arguments used to push Proposition 9, apply only when you (the Commission) think the public should know? Sincerely, JOHN STULL Senator, 38th District P.S. The contributors to many candidates might face "harassment" (e.g., those who opposed Proposition 14), but the reporting law nonetheless applies to all. It is meaningful only if applicable to all candidates and causes. cc: All Legislators cc: FPPC Commissioners [illegible] [illegible] [illegible] ε
